reason: The text contains the phrase 'וַתֶּחֱזַק מִצְרַיִם עַל־ הָעָם' (Egypt strengthened/pressed upon the people), indicating the ruling elite (Egyptians/Pharaoh) exercising forceful pressure on the Hebrew workers (Israelites). Additionally, the context shows Pharaoh urgently commanding the Israelites to leave ('לְמַהֵר לְשַׁלְּחָם') and the Egyptians saying 'we are all dead' ('כֻּלָּנוּ מֵתִים'), suggesting a power conflict where the ruling authority is compelling the workers to depart. This passage represents the tense resolution of the broader Exodus conflict between the ruling elite and the Hebrew workers.
